title: Inside Active: Baillie Gifford’s Coutts, Burns Look Beyond US
summary: Europe and Japan’s earnings outlooks have improved, with double-digit growth expectations, though upgrades are concentrated in technology and energy, while inflation pressures could weigh on valuations. In this episode of Inside Active, host David Cohne, mutual fund and active management analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, and co-host Laurent Douillet, senior equity strategist at BI, speak with Tom Coutts and Lawrence Burns, portfolio managers at Baillie Gifford, and subadvisers on the Vanguard I

title: Alphabet $80 Billion Equity Sale Set to Break Fundraising Record
summary: Alphabet Inc.’s surprise $80 billion sale of equity to fund spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure is expected to become the biggest equity capital markets transaction of all time, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

title: What Went Unsaid at the Shangri-La Dialogue
summary: The Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore brought together top military and civilian leaders to talk defense. But from Iran to Taiwan, what they didn’t say often spoke louder than their prepared remarks.

title: Scotland's ‘Tartan Army' Shells Out for Long-Awaited World Cup Return
summary: Scotland’s last World Cup showing was in 1998 and the pent-up passion is pushing kilt-clad supporters to outspend larger nations.

title: Global Biotech Giants Deepen Ties with China’s Drugmakers
summary: The world’s biggest pharmaceutical companies are signing multibillion-dollar licensing deals with Chinese drugmakers. Here’s why.

summary: Thousands evicted to make way for new developments.

title: Nvidia CEO Gives India Tech Bulls New Ammunition
summary: Huang dismisses fears that AI will decimate the software industry.

title: Palo Alto CEO’s Pay, Now Nearly $100 Million, Is Most Rejected in Corporate America
summary: A majority of Palo Alto Networks investors have voted against the cybersecurity company’s executive pay seven times in 11 years. That’s despite the company’s stock soaring.

title: AI Fuels $280 Billion Cybersecurity Rally as Earnings Test Looms
summary: Investors are about to get a read on the durability of the soaring rally in cybersecurity stocks when two of the industry’s major players report earnings in the coming days.

title: 12 Reasons This Is The Worst Crypto Winter Ever
summary: It’s getting colder and colder.

title: Perplexity Is 'Chip Agnostic,' Says CEO
summary: Computex in Taiwan is putting the spotlight on the companies building the hardware behind the AI boom. One of those companies is Perplexity, which took the stage with Intel to unveil the world's first "hybrid local/server agentic inference orchestrator." Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: True Space Age Is Starting Now, Says Impulse Space CEO
summary: Impulse Space, led by SpaceX alumnus Tom Mueller, just nabbed a $4 billion valuation after the company raised $500 million to scale its 'space tugs,' or spacecraft that can haul satellites across different orbits. Impulse Space Founder and CEO Tom Mueller joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Protesters March in Tirana as PM Defends Kushner Resort
summary: Protesters marched in Albania’s capital against a €4 billion ($4.7 billion) luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump, as opposition widened over the project’s environmental impact and land ownership.

title: Optimum Pushes Apollo, Ares Feud to Brink With Asset Shift
summary: One of the most bitter feuds in distressed-debt investing, between billionaire Patrick Drahi and some of the world’s biggest money managers, has suddenly gotten even more contentious.

title: Tech Stocks Drive S&P 500 to Nine-Day Winning Run: Markets Wrap
summary: A revival of the artificial-intelligence trade kept driving Wall Street momentum, with stocks also rising on hopes for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.

title: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
summary: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

title: Oil Swings Higher as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Deal
summary: Oil edged higher as traders parsed discordant signals on the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Santander Employee Arrested in $85 Million Chile Crime Sting
summary: An employee of Banco Santander’s Chile unit was among 18 suspects detained by police Tuesday as authorities moved against the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua criminal organization allegedly operating in the South American country.

title: Sheinbaum Leans on Private Investors to Fix Beleaguered Grid
summary: Investment in Mexico’s struggling electricity sector is showing fresh signs of life as a slew of deals in power plants, renewables and infrastructure offer momentum to President Claudia Sheinbaum’s push to modernize the grid.

title: Venezuela Hires Hogan Lovells as Counsel for Debt Rework
summary: Venezuela has retained Hogan Lovells US LLP as legal counsel for what is expected to be one of the largest sovereign debt restructurings in decades.

title: US Stocks Gain as Traders Evaluate Middle East, Jobs Data
summary: US stocks were slightly higher on Tuesday as traders parsed the latest developments in the Middle East and fresh employment data.

title: Billionaire Kazakh Brokerage CEO Got SEC Enforcement Warning
summary: The billionaire head of Kazakhstan-based Freedom Holding Corp. has received a so-called Wells Notice from the US Securities and Exchange Commission, a formal warning that the Wall Street regulator may take enforcement action.

title: SpaceX Staffers Prep for Multimillion-Dollar Windfalls by Pushing for VIP Terms
summary: The workers are crafting an IPO wealth playbook that could shape how OpenAI and Anthropic employees cash in.

title: Collector Cars Enter a New Era
summary:
Hagerty Chairman and CEO McKeel Hagerty joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain how the company transformed from a niche insurance provider into a collector-car empire powered by media, memberships, and auctions. He discusses the coming $570 billion wealth transfer in collector vehicles, the surge in Ferrari values, and why electric vehicles could become the next sought-after collectible.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bitcoin’s Inflation-Hedging Promise in Tatters After 36% Plunge
summary: Bitcoin has fallen 36% over the past year and slipped below $70,000 this week, extending a retreat that is undermining several of the arguments that helped carry the cryptocurrency into the financial mainstream.

title: First Brands Had '5 Red Flags,' Says Michael Gatto
summary:
The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands. 
Columbia Business School Professor and Partner at Silver Point Capital, Michael Gatto discusses his take on the First Brands drama, as well as advice for young professionals in credit and distressed markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Venezuela Wants Oil Firms to Supply Their Own Power for Projects
summary: Energy companies heading to Venezuela are being told to bring their own power plants to run their oil and natural gas operations and shield them from frequent blackouts on the nation’s ill-maintained electricity grid.

title: Emerging-Market Currencies Gain as Risk Appetite Rebounds
summary: Most emerging market currencies advanced as hopes of a deal in the Middle East boosted risk sentiment and US data showed a strong labor market.

title: Private Credit Eyes EasyJet Takeover
summary: EasyJet's rejection of a reported Castlelake takeover approach sparks a bigger debate: Are UK-listed companies undervalued? Bloomberg's Silas Brown joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why a private credit giant is interested in aviation deals and reveals that private credit markets remain flooded with cash despite growing geopolitical and inflation concerns. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HPE Rises on Sales Outlook; Victoria's Secret Soars on Profit Beat | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Victoria's Secret (VSXY) shares are soaring after the lingerie maker, now trading under symbol VSXY, reports profit metrics and net sales for its first quarter that topped expectations, including adjusted operating income that was nearly twice the consensus estimate. Management boosted its annual forecasts. 
- Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares are higher in early trading after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that topped estimates, citing massive growth in AI-fueled demand for its servers and networking.
- Marvell Technology (MRVL) is rising after Nvidia Corp.'s Jensen Huang predicted the company will hit a $1 trillion valuation. Huang said Marvell's valuation will soar now that the age of "useful AI has arrived", and was joined by Marvell CEO Matt Murphy on stage at Computex trade show in Taipei. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How the 1987 Crash Changed the Life of Centerbridge's Jeff Aronson
summary: Jeff Aronson was working as a lawyer at LF Rothschild when the 1987 stock market crash put the firm out of business. In this conversation with David Rubenstein, the Centerbridge Co-Founder explains how that setback led him to a career in investing and eventually to Angelo Gordon. Aronson sits down with David Rubenstein for the premiere episode of Bloomberg Wealth Season 6. This interview was recorded April 28 in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Pares Gains as US Jobs Data Reinforces Higher-Rate Bets
summary: Gold pared gains after robust US jobs data reinforced bets that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer, a headwind for the precious metal.

title: Rheinmetall Wins Romanian Defense Contracts Worth €5.7 Billion
summary: Rheinmetall AG, Germany’s largest defense company, will provide €5.7 billion ($6.7 billion) worth of military gear to Romania to help shore up NATO’s Eastern flank, the firm’s biggest international contract package since the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

title: Chipmakers Outperform Software Stocks by Most on Record
summary: Semiconductor stocks rallied on Tuesday while software fell, with the gap between the two sub-sectors of the tech industry the widest on record.

title: Blue Owl Tech Private Credit Fund Is Selling $300 Million Bonds
summary: A private credit fund managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. is looking to sell $300 million of investment-grade bonds, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: UAE’s Strait of Hormuz Pipeline Bypass May Also Include Fuels
summary: The United Arab Emirates is considering building an additional pipeline for refined products that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz and carry fuels to the country’s east coast.

title: Brightline Fields Bankruptcy-Loan Bids While Hoping for a Suitor
summary: Brightline is weighing bankruptcy-loan offers from its largest creditors after the Florida passenger railroad failed to lure a buyer by a self-imposed bidding deadline last month, according to people familiar with the matter.
